top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 135, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
123ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
16,597ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 135 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 135 miles.
16,597ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti. Train it until it feels boring.
135 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Low-cadence force work for 16,597ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 16,597ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: June in Lienz, East Tyrol, Austria can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti’s 135 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti with 16,597ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and VO2max to prepare you for 135 miles of mixed terrain in Lienz, East Tyrol, Austria.
